description: Fix message-server to process messages serially (one at a time) to prevent openclaw session lock contention

userStories.title: Message-server: queue incoming messages and process serially
userStories.description: As a user, I want my messages processed one at a time so openclaw doesn't get concurrent session lock conflicts causing errors and duplicate responses.
acceptanceCriteria: message-server.js maintains a simple async queue (array + processing flag)
When a POST /message arrives and the queue is empty and not processing, process immediately
When a POST /message arrives while already processing, add to queue and respond 200 with { ok: true, queued: true }
After each message completes (success or error), dequeue and process the next one
The queue should have a max depth of 10 — if full, respond 503 with { ok: false, error: 'Queue full' }
Processing is still async relative to HTTP (respond 200 quickly, process in background) — the relay already handles the async response pattern via long-polling
Typecheck passes (this is plain JS, no TypeScript)
userStories.notes: File: docker/tenant-image/message-server.js. The current code calls forwardToOpenclaw() immediately on every request. Add a simple queue: const messageQueue = []; let isProcessing = false; async function processNext() { if (isProcessing || messageQueue.length === 0) return; isProcessing = true; const { text, resolve, reject } = messageQueue.shift(); try { const result = await forwardToOpenclaw(text); resolve(result); } catch(e) { reject(e); } finally { isProcessing = false; processNext(); } }. In the request handler, instead of calling forwardToOpenclaw directly, push to queue. BUT: the relay is polling via long HTTP request — it needs the response inline. So actually the better fix is simpler: use a mutex/semaphore so only one openclaw process runs at a time, but still return the response to the caller when done. Use a simple promise chain as a mutex: let currentProcessing = Promise.resolve(); currentProcessing = currentProcessing.then(() => forwardToOpenclaw(text)).catch(() => forwardToOpenclaw(text)); return await currentProcessing. Actually the cleanest fix: serialize with a simple lock flag and a queue of { resolve, reject, text } tuples.

userStories.title: Remove stale session lock file on container startup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want stale .lock files cleaned up when the container starts so previous crashes don't block new messages.
acceptanceCriteria: In docker/tenant-image/entrypoint.sh, before starting the gateway, remove any stale .jsonl.lock files in /home/agent/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/
This prevents the 'session file locked' error after container restart
userStories.notes: File: docker/tenant-image/entrypoint.sh. Add the find command before the 'exec su -s /bin/bash agent -c ...' line. Must run as root (before dropping to agent user).
